Traders Online 124,958 Page Layout Forex Trader Forex Broker Activity Live Forex Spreads Live Gold Spreads Broker Guide Updates Volatility Now Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee From federalreserve.gov | 16 min ago | 51 comments From federalreserve.gov | 16 min ago | 51 comments The manager turned first to an overview of market developments during the intermeeting period. Asset prices were affected by a number of factors, including developments related to the conflict in the Middle East, continued solid real economic data, higher inflation data, and ongoing investment in artificial intelligence (AI). Optimism around a near-term resolution of the conflict in the Middle East and the announcement of a memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran pushed the oil futures curve and near-term inflation compensation materially lower relative to levels from the time of the April FOMC meeting. Expected policy rates, Treasury yields, the U.S. dollar, and domestic equity prices all rose. Regarding monetary policy expectations, the manager observed that market participants and respondents to the Open Market Desk Survey of Market Expectations (Desk survey) generally expected no change in the target range of the federal funds rate at the June FOMC meeting. Market- and survey-based measures of expected policy rates moved higher over the intermeeting period. In the Desk survey, the median of the modal paths of the federal funds rate implied no changes in the target range through the beginning of 2027 and one rate cut in the second quarter of next year. Market pricing suggested that one rate hike was priced for mid-2027, but the manager noted that these measures were likely boosted, in part, by term premiums. The manager then discussed inflation expectations. He noted that optimism around the Iran conflict pushed market-based measures of expected inflation significantly lower over the period, leaving near-term inflation expectations only moderately higher than they were before the onset of the conflict. Longer-term inflation expectations remained well anchored near the Committee's 2 percent longer-run inflation objective. Fed: Officials saw the labor market remaining stable in the near term. *FED: A FEW SAW CASE FOR RAISING RATES AT JUNE FOMC MEEETING Fed Minutes: Fed staff forecast for inflation in 2026 and 2027 was higher than in April forecast, reflecting the Middle East war and effects of AI buildout. FOMC Minutes Show 'A Few' Fed Members Wanted To Hike In June, 'Majority' Fear Higher Inflation Today's FOMC minutes will be scrutinized for further insight into policymakers' appetite for additional rate hikes and the thinking behind the Committee's hawkish shift at last month's meeting. The minutes are an account of the June 17th meeting and therefore will not reflect subsequent developments, including the softer-than-expected June nonfarm payrolls report or Chair Warsh's appearance at the ECB's Sintra Forum. Since the last FOMC meeting (June 17th), the dollar has strengthened (on the hawkishness) and gold (and bitcoin) mirrored that with sizable declines. Stocks are flat, bond yields are higher (prices down), and oil remains lower, but accelerating in the last couple of days... FOMC Meeting Minutes FOMC Meeting Minutes Recent Comments Lerlor commented Yeah bro i was watching the charts all day long ffghost commented Oh, for sure. The recent Iran/USA escalation + Truths > FED minutes. No question. The one night I'm not up at 3AM he decides to drop a bombshell (literally?). Go figure. Equity markets (well, the /nq anyway) gave it a whole 75 minutes this time though (3-4:15AM) before starting the march back up. ... neosheen commented 3950 soon dychaiky commented Not work brewokz commented thought so too azzamromaneh commented What are your predictions, guys? kevinRK commented Last two days data was weak and gold was falling from 4200. and hit two times lows earlier. Peace deal violated already. Dollar is high now compared to gold. I am expecting buy in gold with SL 4035. chinarian commented No reaction on xau reacted like a gay M0h0t0 commented Dump usd Trader#3693 commented Shorting EU Million97 commented Tweets are stronger than FOMC these days. Trader#B131 commented Damn it! azzamromaneh commented What's wrong with him? Chandan112 commented I think Trump will come at night and pump or dump the market with a personal tweet. What are the thoughts of everyone else? Rajanarora07 commented FFFFAAAAAAAHHHHHHH!! Vancarbon commented Trumps Gob ( mouth- colloquial term ) versus Fed minutes ? The Mad Monk always has the Ace card ; pre meds or fast cycling Bi-polar . What a loose cannon or potentially a genius ( trader - crypto billionaire ) rajkumar.011 commented 3800 is real ? Hilogical commented Aroubd 3972 brother. I dont know when but it will get there Trader#B978 commented nothing moved hahaha natnaeltemes commented buy or sell divirgilioj commented See you around 3800 ffghost commented AI Summary about as long as the minutes (and I don't yet fully trust AI responses), but I am too lazy to summarize myself...... I think the last section is noteworthy though (and is what the FOMC members have been saying): 'cuts are NOT THE DEFACTO PATH' at present. That's a big change from 3 ... Optimism around a near-term resolution of the conflict in the Middle East and the announcement of a memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran pushed the oil futures curve and near-term inflation compensation materially lower relative to levels from the time of the April FOMC meeting. Regarding monetary policy expectations, the manager observed that market participants and respondents to the Open Market Desk Survey of Market Expectations (Desk survey) generally expected no change in the target range of the federal funds rate at the June FOMC meeting. Longer-term inflation expectations remained well anchored near the Committee's 2 percent longer-run inflation objective.
